actions.reasoning: Target-based outlier trimming removes official training rows instead of keeping the full regression signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from notebook-side outlier-removal discussion by converting it into a hard training-row filter.

actions.reasoning: ID is preserved for submission output but removed from the modeling matrix.
actions.context_notes: Competitive notebooks usually keep ID aside for submission and drop it before fitting feature transforms or regressors.

actions.reasoning: Hashing the eight low-cardinality categorical columns discards interpretability and buys little over simpler observed branches.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the notebook-backed categorical-encoding family by replacing explicit label/one-hot handling with a less transparent hashed encoding.

actions.reasoning: Compressing the full feature block to only two principal components is too destructive for this already information-sparse anonymized table.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the observed PCA branch by shrinking the retained component count to an aggressively lossy setting.

actions.reasoning: The official train table contains a fixed set of constant columns that notebooks routinely remove before downstream modeling.
actions.context_notes: The deterministic constant set in the official train file is X11, X93, X107, X233, X235, X268, X289, X290, X293, X297, X330, and X347.
evidence_snippets: one_value_cols = [col for col in train.columns if train[col].nunique() <= 1]
There are total 13 variables with zero variance , therefore they must be dropped.

actions.reasoning: Scaling the identifier treats record order like a meaningful continuous feature instead of dropping the key.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived as a harmful alternative to the benchmark-preferred ID drop.

actions.reasoning: Compact label encoding over the official object columns is the dominant preprocessing branch in the selected notebook corpus.
actions.context_notes: Notebooks usually loop over object dtype columns; on the official files that means X0, X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, and X8. X7 is absent from the released tables.

actions.reasoning: Dropping repeated predictor patterns collapses legitimate training rows that share features but differ in target values.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from notebook duplicate-handling discussion, but redirected to predictor-level deduplication that removes many official training rows.

actions.reasoning: A selected feature-importance branch keeps a compact subset of the wide anonymized feature block before regression.
actions.context_notes: Authored as a model-specific branch because the retained subset depends on the chosen estimator and threshold.

actions.reasoning: Dropping the entire categorical block throws away the only low-cardinality string features instead of encoding them.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the notebook-backed categorical-encoding branch by deleting the source columns instead of converting them.

actions.reasoning: A variance threshold this high strips out many rare but potentially useful sparse indicators from the anonymized binary block.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from notebook-side low-variance pruning ideas, but made overly aggressive for the sparse feature space.

actions.reasoning: Many competitive stacks add a 12-component PCA branch on the repaired feature matrix.
actions.context_notes: Projection rows are model-specific and assume the compact post-repair feature matrix rather than the raw object columns.

actions.reasoning: Treating the submission key as a label-encoded categorical feature preserves identifier leakage instead of removing it.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the good ID-drop and label-encoding branches by redirecting encoding onto the identifier column.

actions.reasoning: Robust scaling appears in non-tree ensemble branches that want a less outlier-sensitive numeric representation.
actions.context_notes: Kept outside the primary denominator because many tree-heavy baselines skip scaling entirely.

actions.reasoning: Treating all two-level sparse indicators as disposable constant-like columns destroys much of the benchmark signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the correct zero-variance pruning step by escalating it to a two-unique-value cutoff over the full feature block.

actions.reasoning: A two-component truncated-SVD branch compresses the feature block too aggressively to preserve the competition signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the observed 12-component SVD branch by collapsing it to an over-compressed setting.

actions.reasoning: Several notebooks expand the low-cardinality categorical block with explicit dummy columns instead of compact label codes.
actions.context_notes: Equivalent core alternative to label encoding; the bank treats both as one effective categorical-repair unit.
evidence_snippets: df = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=["X{}".format(x) for x in range(9) if x != 7])

actions.reasoning: Cutting the wide feature space down to only ten features is an over-pruned importance-selection branch.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the notebook-backed model-importance selection branch by shrinking the retained feature budget too far.

actions.reasoning: RFECV-style recursive elimination appears as another real branch for taming the wide anonymized feature set.
actions.context_notes: Kept in the same model-specific equivalence group as the tree-importance selector because both express downstream feature-pruning strategies rather than deterministic core preprocessing.

features_rfecv = [f for f,s in zip(X, selector.support_) if s]

actions.reasoning: Filtering rows by the identifier exploits ordering artifacts and also drops required prediction rows from the official test table.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from notebook discussion about weak ID trends by turning that observation into a destructive row-filter rule.

actions.reasoning: Discarding the entire sparse binary block leaves only the small categorical slice and destroys most of the benchmark feature signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from notebook feature-pruning themes by escalating them into a full removal of the high-dimensional sparse block.

actions.reasoning: FastICA is another recurring 12-component projection branch in Mercedes stacked solutions.
actions.context_notes: Authored as an alternative projection choice to PCA and SVD rather than as an additional mandatory step.

actions.reasoning: Encoding only part of the categorical block leaves X4, X5, X6, and X8 untreated.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the good label-encoding branch by shrinking the covered column set to a partial near-miss.

actions.reasoning: Truncated SVD is a repeated low-rank branch in stacked Mercedes solutions.
actions.context_notes: Equivalent model-specific alternative to PCA/ICA within the projection family.

actions.reasoning: Dropping every repeated predictor pattern is an even more destructive variant of duplicate-row pruning on this dataset.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the predictor-deduplication mistake by escalating keep=first into dropping every repeated pattern entirely.

actions.reasoning: Rescaling only the categorical code columns treats arbitrary category labels like ordered continuous magnitudes.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the legitimate scaling family by redirecting it to category-code columns where the transformation is semantically weak.

actions.reasoning: Some non-tree branches scale the repaired feature matrix into a bounded range before selection or regression.
actions.context_notes: Alternative model-specific scaling strategy to the robust-scaler branch.

actions.reasoning: A fixed 12-feature recursive elimination branch is much narrower than the notebook-backed RFECV pruning route.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the RFECV branch by hard-capping the retained set to an overly narrow fixed size.

actions.reasoning: One-hot expanding the identifier creates a huge sparse matrix from a column that should have been dropped.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the good ID-drop and one-hot encoding branches by redirecting the expansion onto the unique key.

actions.reasoning: Reducing the repaired matrix to only two ICA components is an overly lossy projection branch.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Derived from the observed ICA branch by collapsing the component count too aggressively.
